doc_layout.repo_security: docs/repo/security/skill.md — documents PATCH /settings and GET .../logs endpoints for GitHub repo security features and job logs

delivery_dual_routing.described_in: docs/periodic-checks.md (point 5 sub-bullets), docs/architecture.md (subsessions subpackage table row)

conventions.changelog_fragments: changelog.d/<id>.<type>.md where type ∈ {feature, bugfix, doc, removal, misc}
conventions.reaction_turn_pattern: assistant replies with exact text matching a known sentinel pattern
conventions.periodic_decision_chat_delivery: Dual delivery: periodic parent inbox + main chat reaction. user_chat subsessions cannot spawn user_chat children.

updated_memory: Repo doc layout: root docs/ is MkDocs-material. Key files: docs/index.md (home), docs/architecture.md (system context, startup flow, subsession concept at lines 31, 54-64, 116-121, 137, 178, 215), docs/periodic-checks.md (full periodic subsession lifecycle including dual-delivery for decision chats at point 5 and nested user_chat prohibition), docs/configuration.md (subsessions config table at lines 223-236), docs/changelog.md (rendered from fragments), docs/contributing.md. No dedicated one-shot/task subsession doc exists — only a passing mention in architecture.md line 31. Changelog fragments live in changelog.d/ and follow towncrier conventions (CI-enforced via `towncrier check`). Memory ledger is a meta-index, not a runtime store.
